title: Samantha SDK — Fields API Surface Design
topic: Samantha fields API — renderField JSON-pass pattern, three levels of control, type-to-renderer map, new artefact staged-write flow, workspace scoping
summary: Samantha fields API: JSON-pass renderField, three control levels, type→renderer map, and workspace-scoped schema resolution.
content:
Overview

The Samantha SDK exposes custom artefact fields to custom app authors through a fields API surface. Rather than requiring app authors to query schema tables or wire up their own form controls, Samantha resolves the field definition, current value, options list, and correct renderer from a single JSON-pass call. The pattern mirrors Jira's type-key-to-renderer mapping but replaces Java plugin indirection with a React component registry and a clean JS/TS API.

Design Principles

  • JSON pass everywhere — all method calls accept a single options object; adding params never breaks existing callers
  • Schema-driven rendering — the type column on the field schema row is the renderer selector; Samantha maps it to the correct React control
  • Three levels of control — data only, schema-aware, or full render — app authors choose their level of coupling
  • New-artefact safe — artefactId: null signals a new unsaved record; Samantha renders with default value and stages the write until save is called
  • Padmin-configured, app-consumed — the padmin defines fields in Vector settings; custom apps consume them without knowing the DB shape

API Surface

Level 1 — Data only

App author manages their own UI. Samantha handles reads and writes against the value table.

// Read a single field value
samantha.fields.getValue({
  artefactId: "uuid",
  artefactType: "defects",
  fieldName: "severity"
})
// returns { value: "critical", type: "select", label: "Severity" }

// Write a value
samantha.fields.setValue({
  artefactId: "uuid",
  artefactType: "defects",
  fieldName: "severity",
  value: "high"
})

// Read the full schema for an artefact type in this workspace
samantha.fields.getSchema({
  artefactType: "defects"
})
// returns [{ fieldName, label, type, required, position, options, defaultValue }, ...]

Level 2 — Schema-aware

App author reads the schema and renders their own components, but lets Samantha handle data fetching and validation.

const schema = await samantha.fields.getSchema({ artefactType: "defects" })

schema.forEach(field => {
  const value = await samantha.fields.getValue({
    artefactId: item.id,
    artefactType: "defects",
    fieldName: field.fieldName
  })
  // render field.type -> own component
  // field.options available for select/multiselect types
})

Level 3 — Full render

Samantha returns a pre-built React component wired to the correct control type, options, validation, and save handler. App author places it — nothing else required.

// Single field
samantha.fields.renderField({
  artefactId: "uuid-or-null",   // null = new unsaved artefact
  artefactType: "defects",
  fieldName: "severity",
  readOnly: false,
  onChange: (val) => {}         // optional — intercept before auto-save
})

// Full dynamic form — all fields in position order
samantha.fields.renderFieldGroup({
  artefactId: "uuid-or-null",
  artefactType: "defects",
  readOnly: false,
  onSave: (values) => {}        // optional — intercept full save payload
})

JSX shorthand (React custom apps)

// Single field — full control
<Samantha.Field
  artefactId={item.id}
  artefactType="defects"
  fieldName="severity"
/>

// Read-only display
<Samantha.Field
  artefactId={item.id}
  artefactType="defects"
  fieldName="severity"
  readOnly
/>

// Full dynamic form in one line
<Samantha.FieldGroup
  artefactId={item.id}
  artefactType="defects"
/>

Type-to-Renderer Map

The type value on the schema row drives which React control Samantha renders. This is the internal registry — app authors at Level 1/2 use the type string to pick their own controls; Level 3 callers get the built-in component automatically.

type valueBuilt-in controlValue column used
textboxSingle-line text inputSTRINGVALUE
richtextLexical rich text editorTEXTVALUE
integerNumber input (integer)NUMBERVALUE
decimalNumber input (decimal)NUMBERVALUE
dateDate pickerDATEVALUE
booleanCheckbox / toggleSTRINGVALUE ('true'/'false')
selectDropdown — single optionSTRINGVALUE
multiselectMulti-select — multiple rowsSTRINGVALUE (one row per value)
userUser pickerSTRINGVALUE (user UUID)
urlURL input with link previewSTRINGVALUE

renderField Options Object — Full Schema

KeyTypeRequiredNotes
artefactIdstring | nullYesUUID of existing artefact, or null for new unsaved record
artefactTypestringYesscope_key from o_artefact_type_registry (e.g. 'defects')
fieldNamestringYesfield_name from the workspace schema
readOnlybooleanNoDefault false — renders display view when true
onChangefunctionNoIntercept value before auto-save. Receives (value, fieldName).
defaultValueanyNoOverride schema default for this render instance
placeholderstringNoOverride schema placeholder for this render instance

New Artefact Flow

When artefactId is null, Samantha operates in staged-write mode:

  1. Resolves schema for the artefactType in the current workspace (subscription)
  2. Renders each field with its schema default value
  3. Stages writes in memory as the user fills in the form
  4. On samantha.fields.commitNew({ artefactType, title, stagedValues }) — creates the core artefact row first, then bulk-inserts all staged field value rows

This prevents orphaned value rows for artefacts that were never saved.

Workspace Scoping

All Samantha field calls are automatically scoped to the calling app's subscription context. App authors never pass a subscription_id — Samantha resolves it from the authenticated session. This means:

  • Two workspaces can have completely different field schemas for the same artefact type
  • Options for select fields are per-workspace — Workspace A's severity options differ from Workspace B's
  • A custom app deployed to multiple workspaces automatically picks up each workspace's field configuration without code changes

Relation to DB Schema

The three-table pattern per artefact type maps directly to the three API levels:

  • o_artefacts_* — resolved by samantha.artefacts.* (core CRUD, not fields API)
  • o_artefacts_*_schema — resolved by samantha.fields.getSchema()
  • o_artefacts_*_field_values — resolved by samantha.fields.getValue() / setValue()

The type column on the schema table is the single source of truth for renderer selection — Samantha reads it at render time and picks the correct control from the internal registry.

Gaps / Open Questions

  • Validation rules (min/max for numbers, regex for text) — not yet in schema table design; likely a validation_json column on the schema row
  • Conditional field visibility (show field X only if field Y = 'critical') — Phase 2 concern
  • Whether renderField returns a React element or a render function — depends on whether Samantha ships as a React library or a framework-agnostic JS SDK
  • Options sub-table design (per-workspace option lists for select fields) — needs a fourth table or JSONB column on schema row

Sources

  1. R013 — Jira Custom Fields Architecture and Scoping (internal research)
  2. Atlassian — Creating a custom field type
  3. Atlassian Forge — Jira custom field type manifest
